description_long: This object consists of a set of four metal traffic barricades, each with a rectangular frame and two wide, flat support feet for stability. Two of the barricades feature prominent yellow and black diagonal striping on their front panels, which are made of solid metal slats, serving as high-visibility warning barriers. The other two barricades have more subdued, rusted metal panels, one with a perforated mesh design and the other with a solid, weathered surface. All barricades have tubular metal side rails and horizontal crossbars, giving them a sturdy, industrial appearance. The surfaces show signs of wear, rust, and exposure to the elements, indicating long-term outdoor use. These barricades are typically used to block or redirect pedestrian and vehicular traffic in urban environments such as streets, construction zones, or event perimeters.
description: A group of four metal traffic barricades, two with yellow-black diagonal stripes and two with rusted metal panels, designed for urban traffic control.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of all four barricades, highlighting the tubular metal frames and the angled support feet.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the two striped barricades with bold yellow-black diagonal patterns and the two rusted metal barricades, one with a mesh panel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profiles and the arrangement of the support feet, with less detail of the front panels.
description_view_3: Rear view revealing the backs of the barricades, including the mesh panel and the weathered surfaces of the non-striped barricades.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal yellow and black stripes, medium width, repeating across the front panels of two barricades.
